message:
fix(distributed): allow verified symbols to replace unverified and tolerate rejected symbols

Two related recovery resilience fixes:

1. RecoveryCollector::add_collected() now checks whether an existing
   symbol for the same ESI is unverified — if so, and the incoming
   symbol is verified, it replaces the unverified entry. This prevents
   a poisoning attack where a malicious peer races to send unverified
   garbage for a given ESI before the legitimate verified symbol arrives,
   permanently blocking recovery of that symbol.

2. StateDecoder no longer fails the entire decoding batch when a single
   symbol is rejected. Previously, SymbolAcceptResult::Rejected caused
   an immediate transition to DecoderState::Failed, aborting all
   remaining symbols in the batch. The fix logs the rejection (behind
   tracing-integration feature flag) and continues processing the rest
   of the batch, since the remaining symbols may be valid and sufficient
   for successful recovery.

Both fixes improve recovery robustness in adversarial or unreliable
network conditions where peers may send a mix of valid and invalid
symbols.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
